content_preview.title: France's First Commercial Floating Offshore Wind Farm Sends Power to the Grid, Clearing Path for Mediterranean Expansion
content_preview.summary: The 30 MW EFGL project off southern France began generating electricity on May 4, marking a milestone for floating offshore wind and anchoring a larger 250 MW follow-on.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

France's first commercial floating offshore wind farm began delivering electricity to the national grid on May 4, 2026, when [Ocean Winds](https://www.oceanwinds.com/news/uncategorized/ocean-winds-delivers-its-first-power-from-floating-offshore-wind-project-in-france/) announced first power from the Éoliennes Flottantes du Golfe du Lion (EFGL) — a 30 MW pilot project sited 16 kilometers off the coast of Leucate and Le Barcarès in the French Mediterranean. The milestone validates a t...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering France's first commercial floating offshore wind milestone. Clear narrative progression from project overview to supply chain, biodiversity innovation, executive statements, and analysis. 830 words within News category range.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources fetched successfully. source-0 (oceanwinds.com): confirmed 30 MW EFGL, May 4 first power, 110,000 MWh/yr, 50,000 inhabitants, 85%/99%/60% supply chain stats, Windram and Hirt quotes verbatim, Biohut/Ecocean, 250 MW EFLO. source-1 (offshorewind.biz May 2026): confirmed Biohut/Ecocean nature-inclusive designation but does NOT mention '32' units — that number is misattributed to this source. source-2 (energyglobal.com): confirmed Windram quote, Hirt quote, 85%/99%/60% supply chain. source-3 (energiesmedia.com): confirmed Almirante quote verbatim. Note: article says Almirante is 'Senior Executive Vice President for Renewable & Flexible Power' but source says 'in charge of Renewable & Flexible Power' — minor paraphrase in title. source-4 (principlepower.com/projects/efgl): confirmed 16 km, Leucate/Le Barcarès, Vestas V164-10 MW, WindFloat, 70-100m water depth, 50,000 inhabitants, Eiffage Métal Fos-sur-Mer fabrication, wet tow to Port-La Nouvelle, AND confirmed '32 Biohut® units' — this is the correct source for that number. source-5 (principlepower.com/news/efgl): confirmed 2 MW WindFloat 1 Portugal, 25 MW WindFloat Atlantic, redeployment to Scotland, 48 MW Kincardine, 2,000 tons primary steel. source-6 (offshorewind.biz Sept 2025): confirmed RTE as grid connection operator. Allowlist warnings: oceanwinds.com (operator company press releases), energyglobal.com (energy industry trade publication), energiesmedia.com (energy sector media), principlepower.com (technology developer) — all credible specialized sources for this topic.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims all verified: 30 MW EFGL, May 4 2026 first power, 16 km offshore Leucate/Le Barcarès, 3×10 MW Vestas V164 turbines on WindFloat semi-submersibles, 70-100m water depth, 110,000 MWh/yr, 50,000 inhabitants, 85% French/99% European/60% SME suppliers, world's first nature-inclusive floating wind farm. TWO MINOR ISSUES: (1) '32 Biohut® units' attributed to Offshore Wind (source-1) but that number only appears in Principle Power source-4 — misattribution of a specific figure to the wrong cited outlet; (2) Almirante's title rendered as 'for Renewable & Flexible Power' vs source text 'in charge of Renewable & Flexible Power'. Both are minor and correctable.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. Analysis section accurately contextualizes the technology's significance without overclaiming. 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags unknowns.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate in recent articles. Covers the May 4 2026 first-power milestone for EFGL — a genuine news event not previously covered.
concerns: Number '32 Biohut® units' is attributed to Offshore Wind (source-1) but only appears in Principle Power (source-4) snapshot — attribution error for a specific figure
Almirante title paraphrased: 'for' instead of 'in charge of' Renewable & Flexible Power
recommendations: Corrections record should document the Biohut attribution error (claim is true, cite is wrong) and Almirante title paraphrase
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate and well-sourced article on an important renewable energy milestone. Two minor attribution issues (wrong outlet cited for 32 Biohut units, slight title paraphrase for Almirante) are correctable via the corrections mechanism. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
